name: Passionflower
summary: Passionflower is best framed as a calming herb with limited human evidence for mild anxiety, pre-sleep relaxation, and nervous tension, with safety screening for sedatives, alcohol, pregnancy, and CNS-depressant use.
description: Passionflower is best framed as a calming herb with limited human evidence for mild anxiety, pre-sleep relaxation, and nervous tension, with safety screening for sedatives, alcohol, pregnancy, and CNS-depressant use.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

claimMap.claim: v9.5: Human evidence includes acute preoperative anxiety and opiate-withdrawal adjunct contexts; general anxiety claims should stay conservative.
claimMap.notes: v9.5: Human evidence includes acute preoperative anxiety and opiate-withdrawal adjunct contexts; general anxiety claims should stay conservative.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: 500 mg oral Passiflora incarnata used 90 minutes before surgery in a preoperative anxiety RCT

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: PubMed PMID 18499602. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 18499602. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: Sedative or CNS depressant medications including benzodiazepines barbiturates and opioids (additive sedation risk)
planned anesthesia or sedation-requiring medical procedures (discontinue at least two weeks beforehand due to additive CNS depressant effects)
pregnancy (traditional uterine-stimulant concerns and insufficient safety data)
MAOI antidepressants (isolated case reports of interaction)
